summary: Sourcey 3.6.3 successfully rebuilt the pinned Gotify Swagger document, preserving 41 endpoints and 22 models while exposing six concrete documentation and publishing gaps with source-vs-generator classification.

sourcey.validate_output: Valid: Gotify REST-API. v2.1.0; Operations: 41; Schemas: 22
sourcey.build_output: Spec: Gotify REST-API. v2.1.0; Operations: 41; Schemas: 22; Time: 4.0s

observations.value: Pinned public Gotify Swagger 2.0 document at commit 8221c0b895fdd1b1b84c0d5784e9422eb314ecb5 under MIT.
Sourcey validate and build commands completed successfully against the pinned snapshot.
41 operations, 22 schemas, 63 generated search entries, and a 4.0 second build.
The source host is localhost and generated api.html contains 289 localhost occurrences; classified as a target-source gap plus a missing Sourcey publish-time safeguard.
24/41 operations lack descriptions, 41/41 lack response examples, and 1/22 schemas lacks a top-level description; classified as target-project documentation gaps.
Complete endpoint navigation, 22 searchable models, operation security rendering, and portable static/search/LLM artifacts worked without template changes.

Sourcey placeholder-host warning proposed at https://github.com/sourcey/sourcey/issues/252; fewer than two Sourcey issues were opened because the remaining gaps are upstream content or already-supported guide configuration.
Correct deploy host guidance, add operation/model prose and response examples, configure task-oriented Markdown guides, and add publish-readiness coverage checks.
